messages.content:
The Statue of Liberty is in New York harbor. It is close to New York City. It rests on an island called Liberty Island. The statue is of a woman. She holds a torch high over her head. The statue is 151 feet tall and weighs 225 tons. A ton is 2,000 pounds. The statue is on a pedestal, or base, that is 150 feet tall. The statue is made of copper and steel. It was made in France. F. A. Bartholdi designed it. It was given to the Unites States as a gift. It was shipped to the Unites States in 1885. It was shipped in pieces. The pieces were put together and the statue was done in 1886. The Statue of Liberty became a symbol for the United States. It is an image that stands for the country. For many people, the statue means freedom. Lots of people visit the statue every year.
What is the statue made of?
A	Copper and steel
B	Gold and silver
C	Bronze and aluminum
D	Iron and titanium
A. Copper and steel

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
If I had enough money, I ....... abroad to improve my English.
A	will go
B	should go
C	would go
D	should have go to
C. would go

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
He tries to give you the ....... that you're out in your car watching the countryside roll by.
A	opinion
B	thought
C	impression
D	interpretation
C. impression

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
The house dog felt he had to ....... himself in the face of all this criticism.
A	display
B	detect
C	defend
D	distend
C. defend

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
I'm ....... you all the way as far as that's concerned.
A	with
B	by
C	at
D	across
A. with

Jenny stared at her new baby sister. The baby was sleeping in the crib in what used to be Jenny's room. Jenny was not sure how she felt about the new baby. She knew her parents were excited. Sometimes Jenny was afraid that they would forget about her. She realized that a new baby requires a lot of attention. She was trying hard not to feel jealous and forgotten. And she had to admit that the baby was very cute. She was lying in the crib, sleeping peacefully. Her hands were balled into two little fists. Jenny decided that she would try her best to be a good big sister. She would help her parents however she could. She would change diapers. She would give the baby her bottles. She would even babysit. Jenny would be helpful instead of jealous. This would be much better for everyone.
What is one thing Jenny decides to do to help?
A	Change the baby's clothes
B	Give the baby a bath
C	Change the baby's diapers
D	The text does not say
C. Change the baby's diapers

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
I'll be home late tonight ....... I have to work late.
A	so
B	because
C	to
D	that
B. because

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
This is a very expensive holiday intended for those with plenty of money as it involves a trip ....... the world.
A	through
B	along
C	about
D	around
D. around

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
It was noisy next door. Our neighbors ....... a party.
A	had
B	were having
C	had had
D	have had
B. were having

Identify the meaning of the word or phrase
adj. square
A	(A) sloping; inclined
B	(B) ready; attentive; quick; agile
C	(C) infinite; boundless
D	(D) having four equal sides enclosing four equal angles
D. having four equal sides enclosing four equal angles

chú ý cho tôi chỗ gạch chân
Identify the meaning of the underlined word or phrase
You know what they said about radio that it had h͟a͟d͟ i͟t͟s͟ d͟a͟y but it's still going strong.
A	stopped being clever
B	stopped being popular
C	stopped being heard
D	stopped being good
B. stopped being popular

Choose the word which is stressed differently from the rest
A	possibility
B	scholarship
C	designate
D	logical
B. scholarship

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
Joan moved to Australia more than a year ago, but she hasn't been able to ....... into that new world yet.
A	suit
B	fit
C	accept
D	harmonize
B. fit

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
Why don't we meet in front of the cinema tomorrow afternoon? What time will ....... you?
A	fit
B	suit
C	accommodate
D	conform
B. suit

Esther wanted a ponytail. Helen had a ponytail. Esther thought it was really cute. Esther's mom always cut her hair short though. Her mom said it was easier to manage. Esther thought it was time she managed her own hair. Helen showed Esther how to make a ponytail. It was easy. Now Esther was practicing braiding. She thought of all the reasons she should have long hair. She wrote down a list. She prepared a little speech for her mom. She practiced her speech in the mirror. She thought she was very convincing. She drew a picture of what she would look like with a ponytail. She found old pictures of her mom with a ponytail. Esther was ready.
What did Esther do to convince her mom to let her have a ponytail?
A	she wrote down a list of reasons
B	she begged and cried
C	she threatened to cut her hair
D	she didn't do anything
A. she wrote down a list of reasons

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
This is ....... blanket I've ever had.
A	soft
B	softer
C	softest
D	the softest
D. the softest

The Statue of Liberty is in New York harbor. It is close to New York City. It rests on an island called Liberty Island. The statue is of a woman. She holds a torch high over her head. The statue is 151 feet tall and weighs 225 tons. A ton is 2,000 pounds. The statue is on a pedestal, or base, that is 150 feet tall. The statue is made of copper and steel. It was made in France. F. A. Bartholdi designed it. It was given to the Unites States as a gift. It was shipped to the Unites States in 1885. It was shipped in pieces. The pieces were put together and the statue was done in 1886. The Statue of Liberty became a symbol for the United States. It is an image that stands for the country. For many people, the statue means freedom. Lots of people visit the statue every year.
What is the name of the island where the Statue of Liberty rests?
A	Manhattan Island
B	Liberty Island
C	Ellis Island
D	Staten Island
B. Liberty Island

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
You've refused to help me now for six months and so I shall now have to take the matter ........
A	on
B	longer
C	far
D	further
D. further

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
He ....... the banana to his twin brother.
A	recommended
B	gave
C	announced
D	suggested
B. gave

để ý gạch chân
dentify the meaning of the underlined word or phrase
K͟e͟e͟p͟ y͟o͟u͟r͟ s͟p͟i͟r͟i͟t͟s͟ u͟p, it may not be as bad as you think.
A	Don't get depressed
B	Carry on drinking
C	Don't let the levels drop
D	Maintain the heights
A. Don't get depressed

Identify the meaning of the word or phrase
v. fetch
A	(A) to govern; to control; to determine; to decide; to decree
B	(B) to establish; to create; to build; to set up; to start a company
C	(C) to go after and bring back; to pick up; to charm
D	(D) to appreciate; to esteem; to respect; to appraise; to assess worth
C. to go after and bring back; to pick up; to charm

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
Do you see the cat ....... is lying on the roof?
A	which
B	where
C	what
D	whom
A. which

Choose the word which has different sound in the part underlined
A	promis͟e
B	devis͟e
C	surpris͟e
D	realis͟e
B. devis͟e
are you sure
Yes, I am sure. The sound of 'i' in the word 'devise' is different from the sound of 'i' in 'promise', 'surprise', and 'realise'.

Identify the meaning of the word or phrase
v. correct
A	(A) to make right; to amend
B	(B) to surround; to confine; to close in; to place an additional document or letter within another
C	(C) to attach; to connect; to unite; to combine; to become a member
D	(D) to control; to dominate; to hold; to own; to have as property
A. to make right; to amend

chú ý gạch chân
Choose the word which has different sound in the part underlined
A	fe͟w͟
B	ne͟w͟
C	thre͟w͟
D	kne͟w͟
D. kne͟w͟

Choose one correct answer to fill in the blank
At the Svelte Health Club, all patrons should have ....... body fat percentage measured before they embark on an exercise regimen.
A	they
B	them
C	their
D	theirs
C. their

Choose the word which is stressed differently from the rest
A	navigation
B	separate
C	offering
D	difference
B. separate

Choose the word which is stressed differently from the rest
A	relative
B	architecture
C	educate
D	edition
